type ApiResponse<T> = { data: T; status: number; message: string; };
const handler = async (req: Request, res: Response) => { const token = req.headers.authorization; if (!token) return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }); };

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; export function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T { const [debouncedValue, setDebouncedValue] = useState<T>(value); return debouncedValue; }
class EventEmitter { private listeners = new Map<string, Function[]>(); on(event: string, cb: Function) { const arr = this.listeners.get(event) ?? []; this.listeners.set(event, [...arr, cb]); } }
Anyone know the ETA on the auth refactor PR? Looks like it's been sitting in review for a week now.
PSA: we're migrating off AWS us-east-1 next Tuesday. All services need to be ready by then. Please coordinate with your teams!
The new onboarding flow is live in staging. Would love some eyes on it before we ship to prod.
Heads up: production incident ongoing. DB connections spiking on replica. On-call is investigating.
Just merged the rate-limiting feature. We went from 500ms avg latency to 80ms. Big win for the team!
Reminder that the Q2 eng all-hands is tomorrow at 2pm. Agenda has been shared in the wiki.
We finally hit 99.9% uptime for the past 30 days. Shoutout to the SRE team for their work on the alerting pipeline.
Breaking change alert: the /v1/users endpoint is being deprecated in favor of /v2/users next sprint. Please update your integrations.
Hot take: we should move our entire infra to Cloudflare Workers. The cold start problem would basically go away.
Interviewing a candidate for senior backend role this afternoon. Anyone have last-minute system design questions they'd recommend?

Monday standup: quick check-in on sprint progress.
Attendees: dev team (6)
Status: on track for release Thursday.
Action items: QA sign-off needed before deploy.
After a long discussion, the team decided to use PostgreSQL instead of MySQL for the new analytics pipeline. The main reason was better support for JSON queries.
The RFC for the new event-driven architecture was approved by the architecture committee. Implementation starts next sprint.
After reviewing the security audit report, it was resolved that all user passwords must be re-hashed using bcrypt with a cost factor of 12.
The team agreed to adopt trunk-based development and retire long-lived feature branches starting next quarter.
Leadership decided to sunset the legacy Python 2 services by end of Q3. All teams must migrate their services by then.
It was agreed during the vendor review that we will not renew the Datadog contract and will migrate to Prometheus + Grafana.
The proposal to open-source the core SDK was approved by the executive team pending legal review. Expected timeline is 6 months.
After debate, the team resolved to keep the monorepo structure rather than splitting into separate packages. The tooling overhead was deemed too high.
The engineering leads decided to enforce mandatory code reviews from at least two senior engineers for all database schema migrations.
It was decided in the incident review that all services must implement circuit breakers before the next production deployment.
The API gateway migration has a hard deadline of March 31. All teams must have their services migrated and tested by then or risk a production freeze.
Compliance report due by EOD today. Please send your sections to the shared doc before 5pm so we can do a final review.
Security patch must be applied to all production servers by Friday. This is a critical vulnerability (CVE-2024-1234).
Performance benchmarks need to be completed due by next Monday. Results will be presented to the board on Tuesday morning.
Vendor contract renewal deadline is approaching — we need a signed decision by Thursday or we lose the current pricing tier.
The quarterly newsletter was published last week. It covers team updates and product highlights.
Just a quick note: I reorganized the shared drive folders so they match our new project structure.
The team had lunch together to celebrate hitting 10k users. Great milestone for everyone involved.
New office chairs arrived today. The ergonomic upgrade should help with the back pain complaints from remote work era.
The company holiday party is scheduled for December 20th at the downtown venue.
We updated the team wiki with the new onboarding checklist for junior engineers joining the platform team.
The coffee machine on the 3rd floor is broken. Facilities has been notified and a replacement is on order.
Happy birthday to our lead designer! Cake in the kitchen at 3pm today.
Reminder to submit your timesheets before end of the month so payroll can process on time.
New parking permits are available at the front desk. Please bring your vehicle registration.
